mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment rates (UNRATE > 10%) signal weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 10%, or the correlation between UNRATE and returns falls below -0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal weakening economic demand, creating a negative feedback loop that drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Both GDPC1 and INDPRO decline but 60-day SPX returns exceed -1.5% in > 40% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) create higher discount rates that reduce equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns during periods of active rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the regression coefficient for FEDFUNDS turns positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO > 100) indicates robust manufacturing activity that supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises but 5-day SPX returns remain below 0.5% in > 70% of cases, or the correlation between INDPRO and returns falls below 0.15 in rolling 6-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST > 550k) and consumer spending (RSAFS > 340k) signals strong demand-side momentum that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: HOUST and RSAFS both exceed thresholds but 20-day SPX returns fall below 0.8% in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes non-significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST > 550k) indicate strong residential construction activity that boosts employment and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds 550k but 20-day SPX returns remain below 0.5% in > 60% of cases, or the correlation between HOUST and returns drops below 0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 130k) signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS exceeds 130k but 5-day SPX returns remain above -0.3% in > 70% of cases, or the regression coefficient for PAYEMS turns positive and significant (p < 0.05).
